meaning: n.<美口>不祥物,白虎星vt.使倒霉
examples: He was beginning to think he was a jinx.
Eg: I think this watch is a jinx to me.
M: Jinx. Jinx can be used as a verb or a noun.
That made me secure after that, and I didn't really worry about the sophomore jinx.
"Most of these guys are too afraid to jinx it, " he said, laughing.
